decisions.title: Prioritize AI/Agent Infrastructure vs. Core Product Stability
decisions.context: Seeing significant investment in AI tooling (AGENTS.md, Claude actions, agentic instrumentation, UI generation agent) while core E2 bugs remain (redirect loops, persistence issues, calculated channels). Need to decide if AI investments align with 'Nail the Core' pillar or if they're premature optimization.

arguments.for: AI agents could accelerate development velocity and improve decision capture through better instrumentation. Strategic moat-building.
arguments.against: Core product has authentication loops and data persistence bugs. 'Nail the Core' means fixing fundamentals first. AI investments won't matter if users can't reliably use the product.

decisions.title: Scale RDS Now or Wait for K8s Upgrade
decisions.context: ENG-8424 proposes scaling prod RDS, ENG-7607 is upgrading k8s to 1.33, and ENG-9278 addresses WAL retention causing OOMs. Infrastructure decisions are interdependent. Need coordinated approach to avoid double-downtime.
decisions.blockedBy: K8s upgrade timeline and RDS scaling impact analysis incomplete

arguments.for: RDS scaling addresses immediate performance issues. Can't wait for k8s upgrade which has longer timeline.
arguments.against: Two major infrastructure changes create risk. K8s 1.33 may change resource requirements, making RDS scaling premature.

decisions.title: Commit to Files in CEL for Canvas or Defer
decisions.context: ENG-9337 starts Files in CEL core implementation. This is foundational for Canvas product vision but significant engineering investment. Need explicit go/no-go based on Canvas adoption metrics and Python Rules traction.

arguments.for: Files in CEL unlocks Canvas differentiation and 'Embed Deeper' pillar. Enables complex analysis workflows customers need.
arguments.against: Python Rules is newer bet. May be duplicative investment. No clear signal that customers are blocked without Files in CEL.

decisions.title: Standardize Downsampling Strategy Across Products
decisions.context: ENG-8790 and ENG-8791 address downsampling configurability and persistence. This affects Explore V2 and Canvas UX. Need product decision: is downsampling user-configurable or auto-optimized? Impacts 'Decisions Captured' metric if users must manually configure.

arguments.for: User control over downsampling gives power users precision. Persisting choices reduces repetitive work.
arguments.against: Auto-optimization reduces cognitive load. Most users don't understand downsampling. Better default = more decisions captured per session.

timeAudit.weekSummary: 60% meeting load - typical for strategy alignment week but limiting IC work on critical bugs
